context: The agent must commit a cross-cutting rename (makeRequest -> sendRequest across 5 files) and a small unrelated typo fix in a TypeScript project. This tests whether the agent groups semantically coupled changes together, separates truly unrelated changes into distinct commits, uses Conventional Commits format correctly, checks git log for the repo's existing commit style, stages specific files rather than using git add ., and documents commits in commit-summary.md.

checklist.name: Rename grouped in one commit
checklist.description: All five renamed files (src/client.ts, src/index.ts, src/middleware.ts, src/retry.ts, examples/basic.ts) appear in the same commit — not spread across multiple commits

checklist.name: Typo fix is separate commit
checklist.description: The utils.ts typo fix is in a different commit from the rename changes

checklist.name: Conventional Commits type prefix
checklist.description: Every commit subject starts with a valid type (feat, fix, perf, docs, refactor, test, build, ci, chore, revert) followed by optional scope and colon

checklist.name: Rename commit uses refactor type
checklist.description: The commit containing the makeRequest->sendRequest rename uses the 'refactor' type (not 'feat' or 'chore'), since it is a non-functional rename

checklist.name: Typo fix uses fix or docs type
checklist.description: The commit for the utils.ts comment typo fix uses 'fix' or 'docs' type

checklist.name: Lowercase imperative description
checklist.description: All commit message subject descriptions start with a lowercase letter and use imperative mood (e.g. 'rename', 'fix', not 'Renamed', 'Fixed')

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: No commit message subject line ends with a period

checklist.name: Description ≤72 chars
checklist.description: Every commit message subject line is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Specific files staged
checklist.description: commit-summary.md lists individual named files per commit entry (not a catch-all like 'all files'), indicating specific file staging rather than git add .

checklist.name: Prerequisite ordering
checklist.description: In commit-summary.md, the rename commit (touching src/client.ts) appears before the typo-fix commit, OR they are independent and either order is acceptable — the point is that the rename is not split such that callers are updated before the definition

checklist.name: commit-summary.md created
checklist.description: A file named commit-summary.md exists inside the fetchkit/ repository created by setup.sh and contains at least two sections documenting the commits made (hash, subject, files)
